seoTitle: Clarendon vs Clareville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clarendon and Clareville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clarendon and Clareville.
heading: Clarendon vs Clareville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clarendon, New South Wales and Clareville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clarendon has a lower overall climate risk score (46/100) compared to Clareville (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clarendon or Clareville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clarendon has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Clareville scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clarendon scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Clareville scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clarendon or Clareville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clarendon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
